metadata.falPrompt:
Create a high-resolution fantasy tactical RPG monster status sheet UI template, front-facing, perfectly flat 2D game interface, no perspective tilt.

Style:
Medieval dark fantasy parchment interface with aged cream paper, dark carved wood borders, antique brass/gold filigree corners, subtle ink wear, clean readable panel divisions, inspired by classic tactical RPG enemy inspection screens and illuminated manuscript borders. Elegant, functional, and readable.

Canvas:
Square 1024x1024 image. Full monster sheet fills most of the canvas with a small dark neutral margin around it. Crisp pixel-art inspired edges, clean grid alignment, symmetrical panel structure.

CRITICAL TEXT RULE:
Do not bake in any dynamic data text. Do not include monster names, level numbers, family names, stat values, XP text, coin text, drop names, item names, ability names, trait names, resistance names, or placeholder words.
Only permanent section headers are allowed:
- Stats
- Resistances
- Abilities
- Traits & Tags
- Rewards & Drops

Everything else must be empty UI space, blank lines, blank boxes, or icon slots. No fake readable text. No lorem ipsum. No numbers. No labels inside the reward rows.

Layout:
Top-left profile panel:
- Large ornate empty portrait frame.
- Empty nameplate below portrait, completely blank.
- Two blank info rows below for level and family, but do NOT write “Level” or “Family”.
- Each info row should have a blank line and/or blank value box only.

Top-center stats panel:
- Header may say “Stats”.
- Rows for stats should be visually indicated with blank lines and empty value boxes, but do NOT write HP, MP, CT, Power, Defense, Speed, Strength, Intellect, Faith, or XP.
- Use clean horizontal guides and empty right-side value boxes.
- Leave generous spacing so game text can be added later.

Top-right resistances panel:
- Header may say “Resistances”.
- Six evenly spaced blank resistance rows.
- Each row has a square icon slot and a long empty value box/bar.
- No resistance names, no numbers, no symbols.

Bottom-left abilities panel:
- Header may say “Abilities”.
- Four large empty ability cards in a 2x2 grid.
- Each card has a small empty icon slot and a wide blank name bar.
- Leave the body of each card empty for dynamic descriptions.
- No ability names or placeholder text.

Bottom-middle traits panel:
- Header may say “Traits & Tags”.
- Five or six compact blank rows.
- Each row has a small empty icon slot and a blank line.
- No trait names, no icons with readable letters, no symbols that imply specific traits.

Bottom-right rewards panel:
- Header may say “Rewards & Drops”.
- Six stacked blank reward/drop rows.
- Each row has an empty square icon slot and a blank horizontal line or empty text box.
- IMPORTANT: do not write XP, Coin, Drop name, item name, or any other reward text.
- Do not include coin icons, item icons, swords, bags, gems, or specific reward artwork.
- All reward icons must be empty placeholder slots only.

Visual requirements:
Clean readable spacing. Strong panel separation. No overlapping frames. No cramped layout. No dense texture behind text areas. Interiors should be pale parchment with subtle guide lines. Borders may be ornate, but text regions must stay simple and usable. Warm parchment, dark walnut wood, muted antique gold, and thin black ink outlines.

Technical requirements:
Game UI asset, empty template only. Sharp 2D raster image. No character or monster illustration. No readable dynamic labels except the allowed section headers. All panels must align to a clean grid so dynamic game labels and values can be drawn over them later.
No XP text, no Coin text, no Drop name text, no reward labels, no item names, no stat labels, no HP text, no MP text, no CT text, no Level text, no Family text, no numbers, no fake words, no lorem ipsum, no readable placeholder text, no baked-in values, no coin icon, no item icon, no sword icon, no bag icon, no trait letters, no AI letters, no monster art, no character art, no 3D perspective, no blurry UI, no overlapping panels, no cramped text areas., 16-bit pixel art, SNES-era detailed sprites, rich palette
